description: Adding, removing, or modifying entries in the capability registry JSON for dynamic retrieval.
constraints: Must preserve JSON schema; all entries must have source_ids referencing BENCHCORE-DOC documents.

description: Updating coverage CSVs, source hashes, evidence records, and visual inventory.
constraints: Hashes must be either valid SHA-256 or literal 'placeholder'; all PDF IDs must exist in the inventory.

description: Creating or modifying evaluation datasets, expected metrics, test fixtures, and calibration datasets.
constraints: Fixtures must conform to their respective JSON schemas; must not introduce bias toward specific labels.

description: Training or updating a lightweight classifier (e.g., logistic regression, small SVM, embedding-based ranker) operating on frozen core LLM outputs.
constraints: Classifier must not modify core LLM weights; must be evaluated against deployment gates before promotion.

description: Updating reranking weights, score thresholds, max_results, or fallback strategies in retrieval reranking policy.
constraints: Must preserve the reranking policy JSON schema; changes must be logged with before/after values.

description: Adjusting reliability scores, confidence calibration curves, position bias correction parameters, or smoothing coefficients.
constraints: Must not increase the mean reliability score beyond 1.0; calibration must be validated on held-out feedback events.

description: Recording, aggregating, and analyzing feedback events for diagnostics and drift detection.
constraints: Must not modify core model behavior; aggregation must be statistically sound (e.g., bootstrap CIs reported).

description: Producing confusion matrices, bootstrap interval reports, regression dashboards, and metric summaries.
constraints: Artifacts must be reproducible from logged evaluation runs; must not modify model state.

description: Adding or updating hypotheses, candidate tests, and negative knowledge records in inverse_science_alignment.
constraints: Hypotheses must be traceable to observations; tests must be falsifiable; negative knowledge must cite evidence_against.
